framework.name: Fraud Liability Trichotomy
framework.description: Verifiable Intent enables issuers to distinguish three categories: (1) fraudulent/unauthorized agent, (2) legitimate transaction consumer regrets, (3) agent error — authorized but incorrectly executed. This is the liability classification framework that partially addresses the EFTA gap.
framework.raw_quote: Issuers using Verifiable Intent can distinguish between: A fraudulent transaction... A legitimate transaction the consumer now regrets. An agent error: the transaction was authorized but executed incorrectly.

source.title: What Is Mastercard Verifiable Intent? Agentic Commerce Trust Layer ...
